_notes: Conduction Transfer Function (CTF) coefficients per construction
type. These are the Y / Z / Φ series of a 24-term truncated CTF
expansion (ASHRAE Handbook Fundamentals 2021 Ch.18 §RTS).
STING's BlockLoadEngine RTS implementation has two layers:
Tier-1 (Phase 187f): pick one of three published RTF tables
(Light / Medium / Heavy) — instant, no CTF.
Tier-2 (Phase 187g): interpolate between the three tables by zone
average ThermalMassKJperM2K — better, still
no CTF math.
Tier-3 (Phase 187h): when a construction TYPE has CTF coefficients
in this file, derive the per-zone RTF by
impulse-response convolution of the Y-series.
Most rigorous shipped, no Laplace inversion
at runtime (coefficients are pre-tabulated).
Sources: ASHRAE Handbook Fundamentals 2021 Ch.18 Tbl 24 examples +
ASHRAE Loads Toolkit 1.4 published CTF series. Coefficients are
dimensionless impulse-response coefficients; they sum to the wall's
thermal admittance.
Project teams add custom construction types via:
<project>/_BIM_COORD/ctf_coefficients.json

constructions.label: Double-glazed argon-filled 16 mm
constructions.source: Generic — glazing has effectively no lag
